texto: Big food companies make hot dogs with mechanically separated meat (msm) that, as described matter-of-factly by the [USDA], is "a paste-like and batter-like meat product produced by forcing bones with attached edible meat under high pressure through a sieve or similar device to separate the bone from the edible meat tissue.". I read that and wanted to unread it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jennifer Reese: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Big food companies make hot dogs with mechanically separated meat (msm) that, as described matter…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, food, food-industry pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de food-industry e gross.

texto: Big food companies flatter us by telling us how busy we are and they simultaneously convince us that we are helpless. I am moderately busy, but not all that helpless. Neither are you.
